top_posts.text:
@femalepentimento’s (Female Pentimento) magical, reorienting photo-based images explore nature, the metaphysical and diverse ways of inner healing. ⁣⁣
⁣⁣
“My hope is that the things I create convey a sense of divine beauty and sacredness, reminding us of the interconnectedness of all things,” says the nonbinary artist, whose aesthetic practice augments the immediacy of the captured image through layering, collage and digitally painting on top of photographs. ⁣⁣
⁣⁣
“The feminine spirit of nature, as the originator of life, holds a special place in my heart. I see my artistic process as a way of honoring this spirit and sharing its radiance with others. I’m interested in different natural phenomena — rainbows, sunlight, lightning. I love how symbolically loaded these elements are throughout cultures and art history. Light forms and light portals are probably one of the most magical elements I experiment with in my images. They border on the fantastical and imagine a world of what could be.” ⁣⁣
⁣⁣
Art by @femalepentimento

🔥 Wanna dance?

K-pop girl group @twicetagram (TWICE) made a recent trip to Los Angeles where they showed off the choreo to their new single, “SET ME FREE.”

Music by @twicetagram

“I am proud to identify as a Bolivian woman through my cultural descent, and to be able to do that with what I like the most, which is skateboarding,” says graphic designer @huara__ (Huara Medina Montaño), who is a descendant of the Indigenous Quechua people and a member of the nine-woman @imillaskate (ImillaSkate) collective. 
 
“ImillaSkate seeks to value our culture and identity through our sport, giving a message of inclusion, where we can all skate and be a part, regardless of how you look or how you dress. 
 
By challenging a stigma about the woman in a skirt and breaking stereotypes by being skating cholitas, we want to send a message of empowerment: women are capable of achieving what they set out to do.” 
 
Video by @imillaskate 
Music by @kendricklamar

“I’m neither a coward nor the bravest. I’m just brave.” —Model and creator @xiaoyangbure (Xiaoyang) 
 
“In junior high, I realized I was different. I began concealing myself. As long as I wrapped myself up tightly enough, no one would know, but this caused me to miss out on so many chances to dress up,” says Xiaoyang, who has worn a prosthetic leg for 20 years. 
 
Xiaoyang challenged herself and shared her first post — a selfie wearing a dress — and received affirmation, plus a message from jewelry brand @yvmin_official (YVMIN). “They wanted to design a more attractive covering for me. We met, and soon I had a ‘new skin.’” 
 
“I’ve accepted myself. I wear whatever I like. Sharing my outfits and daily experiences is how I express myself. My favorite looks express a kind of state of being, one that exudes an aura of cozy confidence.” 
 
Photos by @xiaoyangbure

commercial_posts.text:
Photo by @yesfira
Becoming a hairdresser was a natural evolution for 21-year-old Yesfira Sidorenko (@yesfira). “In ninth grade, I got interested in doing hair from watching online tutorials, and started practicing on my little sisters,” says Yesfira, who was born in Latvia and lives in Alaska. “After high school, I decided to go to cosmetology school, got a job as a hairdresser, and this is where I am today.” Braids were one of Yesfira’s earliest styles she worked to perfect, and she’s stuck with them through the years, adding in more complex twists, waves and updos for weddings and special occasions. “If I were going to #prom today, I would wear my hair in a loose mermaid wave, with my hair tucked to one side with a clip.”

recent_posts.text:
Hippity-hoppity, this is now my property. 🧦🐤

Jumping into the weekend — and a new pair of socks — with @_with_coco (Coco), a curious and playful black-headed caique.

#WeeklyFluff 🤗

Video by @_with_coco
Music by Mzyika Sound

“Hi, we’re Flyana Boss, and we want to wish hip-hop a happy 50th.”

“We’re a bestie rap duo, but we’re also pop stars. Every genre, we believe, has been influenced by hip-hop. It’s the motherland! So, it’s only right we pull from and rep them, too,” says Folayan and Bobbi, who together make up @flyanaboss (Flyana Boss).

“Shout out Missy, she’s our queen. Shout out to all the hip-hop OGs we’ve ever referenced. Shout out to Nelly, to Onika Maraj and ALL the greats. We wouldn’t be us without you. We’re building on everything that came before. And we want to inspire others to chase their dreams with a true love, true friend right beside them.”

The duo’s track “You Wish” and signature running videos have taken them viral, resonating particularly with a Black girl audience, and have helped carry forward the legacy of women of hip-hop.

“We should be celebrating the women of hip-hop! We feel like they’re unsung heroes who have ALWAYS been a part of the story and they haven’t always got the shine that they’re due. So, cheers to all those who paved the way for us to be here doing what we do. We are SO blessed and appreciative.”

Video by @flyanaboss

Kicking it. 🔥

#InTheMoment 

Video of @kamel_reon and @jamaaa_n, @sseowu, @barbieofficia1, @daehan_yoon by @s.jin0119 
Music by @realstraykids

Boozhoo (hello).

“My goal is to equip Indigenous youths with digital literacy skills needed to protect our communities, and I’m doing it with free robots that speak Indigenous languages.” —Indigenous robotics inventor @danielleboyer (Danielle Boyer)

Danielle is the founder of the The STEAM Connection, a youth-led charity that has reached more than half a million young people through technical education with an emphasis on protecting Indigenous languages.

“Language is vital to preserving Indigenous cultures and identities, but even my own language, Ojibwemowin, is considered an endangered language,” says Danielle, who is Ojibew and an enrolled member of the Sault Ste. Marie Tribe.

“I designed SkoBot, a wearable, customizable and interactive language-learning robot for Indigenous youths that senses motion and speaks. It is a free language revitalization and STEM educational tool for Indigenous youths to make their own.

I was taught that everything I do should be to support future generations. Many Indigenous youths are left behind not only from basic educational opportunities, but from being able to pursue the future we want. I see 21st century digital skills as a pathway to opportunity and to have the skills to uplift and support our communities.”

Photos by @danielleboyer

French artist @ayanakamura_officiel (Aya Nakamura) needs no intro or hype squad. But, it definitely helps to have both 🙌

The Mali-born musician is one of the headliners at this year’s Lollapalooza Paris. Here’s #10Things with Aya ahead of her performance at the festival.

1. The look of someone ready to headline @lollapaloozafr in Paris.
2. When the people who make you look good also look good.
3. Aya’s musicians ready for the show 🎶
4. Decisions, decisions…👡✨🌈
5. The outfit has been decided. And the choice was perfect.
6. En route! Pre-show posin’ headed to the stage. ✨
7. Is it a festival in Paris without an Eiffel Tower replica?
8. Squad come through.
9. A fire set list 🔥
10. It’s showtime.

“Being fearless is an integral part of our identity and it feels like a natural way to describe who we are.” —K-pop girl group @le_sserafim (LE SSERAFIM)

LE SSERAFIM is an anagram for the phrase I’M FEARLESS. “LE SSERAFIM gives away our identity, but also our albums explain us well — FEARLESS, ANTIFRAGILE and UNFORGIVEN. We tend to not hesitate as a group in trying new things out when we are faced with something that we feel is worth trying.”

LE SSERAFIM formed in May 2022 as HYBE’s first all-female group. One year later, its five members — KIM CHAEWON, SAKURA, HUH YUNJIN, KAZUHA and HONG EUNCHAE — are at the forefront of a new generation of strong girl groups taking K-pop by storm. Their recent album release UNFORGIVEN, which features legendary Chic co-founder Nile Rodgers on the title track, sold over a million copies on its first day.

“We are dedicated to bettering ourselves as artists while not backing down to others when moving forward because it is our own path. We want to demonstrate through our music and with the path we take as LE SSERAFIM that everyone can achieve their dreams and pursue their passions.”

Video and music by @le_sserafim

There’s more than one way to charge a phone — take it from these guys. 📱🔌👏⁣⁣
⁣⁣
Check out our story for more amazingly ingenious things that made us smile this week.⁣⁣
⁣⁣
Reel by @thatsamazing

All aboard the #WeeklyFluff express. 🚂⁣
⁣
Meet Adoong, Atang, Rene, Viva, Pho, Angcom, Coating, Hacoong, Ette, Piki and Shana who make up the one big (little) @hugbears family.⁣
⁣
Reel by @hugbears 
Music by Mark Ross

“Este 2022 nunca pero nunca pero nunca lo olvidaré. ❤️” — @badbunnypr (Bad Bunny)

#2022Recap

Winston (@winston_britishboy) is all about his regular, post-lunch cat nap. “He loves sleeping on any hard surfaces and ignores all the soft pillows that we bought for him,” says his human Ployvanas.

#WeeklyFluff

Reel by @winston_britishboy

Spend the day in La, La, La Los Angeles with the iconic @lalalalisa_m. ✨✨

You may know her from the South Korean music group BLACKPINK (@blackpinkofficial), but this year marked the singer and rapper’s solo debut with the release of her single album “LALISA.”

Join her for an action-packed day in sunny California. ☀️🌴💛

Reel by @lalalalisa_m
Music by @lalalalisa_m

Fly guy 🐸

Meet Zamin (@gegul_hee), a 3-year-old White’s tree frog who is quick on the draw.
#WeeklyFluff

Reel by @gegul_hee

ChinCHILLIN.😉

Meet Philly (@phillychinchilly), a sweet and patient chinchilla. “Phil appears to really enjoy attention and dressing up and holding signs or objects,” says his human, Michelle. “Chinchillas typically wink when they are content or are acknowledging a communication from their owner. His frequent winks are likely the result of the bond we have formed.” 

Reel by @phillychinchilly
Music by @drdre

Unstoppable!!!⁣
⁣
Brazilian football creator Iran Ferreira (@luvadepedreiro) may play on a clay field with thorns and weeds, but his serious skills and long-range shooting gave him a global audience. His videos have been shared by some of the biggest football clubs in Europe, inspiring some of world’s top players. “I record videos at a place near my house,” says Iran, who lives in the small village of Tábua in Bahia. “I work in the fields in the mornings and record videos in the afternoons.”⁣
⁣
Despite early negative comments about Iran’s penchant for wearing construction gloves, he held true to his signature style — and catchphrase “Receba” (take that) — and became a one-man footballing meme. “Football helped me overcome difficulties and humiliation and turn them into motivation to become the best player in the world. The thing I enjoy the most about playing is scoring goals.”⁣
⁣
Reel by @luvadepedreiro

Here comes the sun.⁣
⁣
Meet Fawkes (@concerningparrots), a naturally affectionate 5-year-old sun conure parrot who loves to squish and cuddle.⁣
⁣
#WeeklyFluff⁣
⁣
Reel by @concerningparrots 
Music by @thebeatles

🔥🔥🔥 We’re on set with South Korean artist @uarmyhope (j-hope) for his new single “Arson.” 
 
“I have been very passionate about my work since I was young, and my passion is hot as fire — so I picked fire for the theme of this song.” 
 
Music by @uarmyhope

Artists @arrdeegram (ArrDee) and @thebugzymalone (Bugzy Malone) have a new single coming out that would never have happened without DMs. 🔥

“He was always someone that I was a massive fan of,” says ArrDee. “I thought about just kind of shooting the shot, and I thought, why not?”

#Seen is our new series all about what’s possible when you connect in DMs.⁣
